page: Hetzner Cloud cax11 is an entry-level, Arm-based virtual server powered by the Ampere Altra processor family. Utilizing the arm64 architecture, this instance features 2 shared vCPUs mapped to 2 physical Neoverse-N1 cores running at 2.0 GHz. It is equipped with 4.0 GB of system memory, providing approximately 1.95 GB of RAM per core, and includes 40 GB of local SSD storage. The virtualization is managed via the QEMU hypervisor.

Performance benchmarks indicate that the cax11 is positioned for lightweight workloads. In stress-ng testing, single-core CPU performance is rated as average with a score of 1821.28, while multi-core performance drops to a weak rating of 3610.67. Memory performance is similarly constrained, with a poor rating in small-block read-only bandwidth at 131.92 GB/sec and weak ratings for large-block read and write operations. Cryptographic operations are supported via hardware-accelerated AES, though other advanced instruction sets like AVX or AMX are absent due to the Arm architecture.

This server profile offers a highly cost-efficient entry point for resource-light applications, trading raw processing power and memory bandwidth for low resource density costs. It is best suited for low-traffic web serving, basic text processing, lightweight Redis caching, and simple development or testing environments that do not require high-performance multi-threaded execution.
description: Hetzner Cloud cax11 is an entry-level, Arm-based virtual server utilizing the QEMU hypervisor. It features 2 shared vCPUs from an Ampere Altra Neoverse-N1 processor running at 2.0 GHz, paired with 4.0 GB of RAM and 40 GB of local SSD storage. Benchmark data indicates average single-core CPU performance with a stress-ng score of 1821.28, but weak multi-core and memory bandwidth capabilities. Lacking advanced vector extensions and dedicated GPUs, this instance represents a highly cost-efficient option for lightweight, non-intensive workloads. It is best suited for basic web serving, simple text processing, small Redis deployments, and general-purpose development tasks that do not demand high computational density.
og_description: Hetzner Cloud cax11 is an Arm-based virtual server featuring 2 shared Ampere Altra vCPUs, 4.0 GB of RAM, and 40 GB of SSD storage, offering a cost-efficient solution for lightweight, general-purpose workloads.

tagline: An entry-level, Arm-based virtual server with shared Ampere Altra cores and local SSD storage for budget-conscious, lightweight workloads.
